intro: Fagara nemoralis is a species of plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the West Indian asafoetida or Caribbean asafoetida. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Fagara nemoralis include limonoids, flavonoids, and coumarins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Fagara nemoralis is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical ointments and creams to take advantage of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
